title: Add a tiny sequence-model expert and blend only its OOF ranks
summary: A recent F1 pit-stop study found sequence models, especially Bi-LSTM, outperform other deep architectures for pit timing because they capture stint history and temporal dependencies that trees compress poorly.
rationale: A recent F1 pit-stop study found sequence models, especially Bi-LSTM, outperform other deep architectures for pit timing because they capture stint history and temporal dependencies that trees compress poorly. Your current stack is almost entirely boosted-tree plus post-hoc smoothing, so a lightweight sequential expert could add orthogonal signal without replacing the main tabular models.
implementation_hint: Sort by `Year,Race,Driver,Stint,LapNumber`; train a small Bi-LSTM or TCN on per-driver stint sequences using only causal per-lap inputs and masks; emit next-lap pit probabilities OOF; use it only as a third ensemble expert via rank blending with XGB/Cat. Keep windows short (for example last 6-12 laps) and compare pure-driver sequences vs driver+race-lap context tokens.
expected_effect: Small but real lift if the gain comes from history shape rather than single-row state; most likely helps edge cases near pit windows where recent pace/degradation trajectory matters.
risk: Neural expert may overfit sparse drivers/races, and grouped CV can still flatter it if sequence boundaries are mishandled. Use strict chronology inside each fold and no future padding.
